key_themes: AI chip partnerships reshaping smartphone and memory landscape
Mixed Q1 earnings — consumer/pizza disappoints, telecoms and steel beat
Iran-Strait of Hormuz tensions cap equity gains; oil pushes higher

stocks.discussion_driver: Qualcomm surged 8-13% after analyst Ming-Chi Kuo reported that OpenAI is partnering with Qualcomm and MediaTek to develop AI smartphone processors targeting 300-400M annual units for a 2028 device launch. The move adds a new AI-device silicon revenue stream for Qualcomm ahead of its Q2 earnings report on April 29, where investors will scrutinize guidance given the looming Apple modem exit and near-term earnings headwinds.
bull_case: The OpenAI-MediaTek partnership positions Qualcomm as the preferred on-device AI chip provider, leveraging its Snapdragon platform's existing inference capabilities; if production targets 300-400M units by 2028, this could more than offset the Apple modem revenue headwind and represent a new multi-billion dollar revenue stream.

Qualcomm's growing diversification into Windows PCs (Snapdragon X Elite), automotive, and IoT reduces smartphone dependence; 24/7 Wall St. sets a bull-case target of ~$184, implying 23% upside, with the AI device tailwind as the primary re-rating catalyst.
bear_case: Apple's transition to in-house modems means Qualcomm supplies only ~20% of iPhones in FY2026, declining to zero by 2027, removing a once-reliable and high-margin revenue stream that will weigh on near-term results.

The OpenAI partnership remains speculative with a 2028 mass-production timeline and no official confirmation from either OpenAI or Qualcomm; if Q2 earnings guidance on April 29 disappoints or the deal falls through, the recent 8-13% surge could unwind rapidly.

stocks.discussion_driver: POET plunged ~46% after Marvell Technology (which acquired Celestial AI) cancelled all purchase orders, alleging POET violated confidentiality obligations by publicly disclosing order details and shipping information. The crash is a dramatic reversal: the stock had surged sharply the prior week when news emerged that its Optical Interposer technology was being integrated into Marvell's AI hardware stack, only to collapse when Marvell walked away entirely.
bull_case: POET's Optical Interposer platform addresses a genuine structural need for photonic integration in AI data centers; a separate $5M purchase order from another undisclosed technology company confirms the customer pipeline extends beyond Marvell and that the technology has validated commercial appeal.

At current prices ~47% below the 52-week high set just days ago, POET is deeply discounted relative to its recent valuation; if the company successfully engages alternative hyperscaler or OEM customers, recovery could be substantial given the secular AI photonics tailwind.
bear_case: The loss of Marvell/Celestial AI orders removes the primary near-term revenue catalyst and raises serious questions about POET's commercial execution — specifically its ability to honor standard confidentiality obligations, a basic expectation for any enterprise technology partner.

The alleged breach creates lasting reputational risk that may deter future strategic partners; POET's limited financial resources provide minimal runway to survive prolonged revenue shortfalls while attempting to rebuild trust with potential customers.

stocks.discussion_driver: Domino's fell ~10% after Q1 2026 US same-store sales grew only 0.9%, badly missing the 2.3% analyst consensus; adjusted EPS of $4.13 also missed the $4.31 estimate. Management lowered full-year same-store sales guidance to 'positive low single digits' for both US and international segments, citing intensifying macro and geopolitical uncertainty as headwinds that are likely to persist into Q2.
bull_case: Despite the soft same-store number, Domino's 'Hungry for MORE' strategy drove 3.5% revenue growth and 9.6% operating income growth in Q1 2026, demonstrating the underlying franchise model remains structurally sound and that the miss reflected macro conditions rather than a loss of brand competitiveness.

At ~26% below its 52-week high and a trailing PE of ~21x near a historical trough, the stock offers a potentially attractive entry for patient investors; 24 analysts maintain a Buy consensus with an average PT of $473 implying ~29% upside from current levels.
bear_case: US same-store sales of +0.9% fell far below the +2.3% consensus, and management's guidance reset to 'positive low single digits' signals macro headwinds are intensifying — value promotions that drove 2025 strength are now lapping tough comparisons with no clear near-term catalyst to reaccelerate growth.

International same-store sales declined 0.4% ex-FX, and the Domino's Pizza Enterprises (DPE) franchise drag — the brand's largest global franchisee — remains unresolved, constraining any near-term global growth recovery and adding a second ongoing headwind to the top-line narrative.

stocks.discussion_driver: Verizon gained ~3.5% after Q1 2026 adjusted EPS of $1.28 beat the $1.20 estimate by 7.6% year-over-year, and the company raised its full-year 2026 adjusted EPS guidance to $4.95-$4.99, above the prior $4.91 analyst consensus. The quarter also delivered Verizon's first positive Q1 postpaid phone net adds since 2013, with CEO Hans Vestberg declaring the operational turnaround 'officially underway.'
bull_case: Verizon's first positive Q1 postpaid phone net adds since 2013 and raised EPS guidance of $4.95-$4.99 confirm the operational turnaround is gaining genuine traction after years of subscriber losses, validating management's transformation strategy with hard subscriber data.

Trading at just 11.4x trailing earnings with an approximate 6.5% dividend yield, Verizon offers compelling income with meaningful capital appreciation potential as the turnaround thesis continues to be validated by quarterly execution.
bear_case: Despite the Q1 beat, 10 analysts recently revised near-term earnings estimates downward, reflecting concerns about intensifying wireless price competition and potential macro softening dampening consumer upgrade cycles in the quarters ahead.

Verizon's massive debt load from spectrum acquisitions constrains capital allocation flexibility; with free cash flow largely committed to dividends and debt service, the company has limited capacity to respond aggressively to competitive threats or accelerate 5G infrastructure investment.

stocks.discussion_driver: Micron surged ~5% after Melius Research initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $700 price target, representing ~41% upside from prior levels, citing sustained HBM demand powering AI data centers through 2030. Analyst Ben Reitzes highlighted that all of Micron's 2026 HBM output is already contracted under long-term agreements and that the compounding nature of AI memory demand — driven by growing model complexity and AI agent proliferation — has durability the market has not yet fully priced in.
bull_case: Melius Research's $700 PT is underpinned by structural shifts: Micron's entire 2026 HBM capacity is pre-sold under long-term contracts, Nvidia selected Micron as a key HBM supplier for its Blackwell GPU platform, and AI model complexity is driving compounding memory bandwidth demand projected to persist through at least 2030.

Despite breaking to all-time highs, Micron trades at just ~6x forward earnings — a steep discount to the ~25x semiconductor sector average — creating significant multiple expansion potential as Wall Street reprices AI memory as a secular growth story rather than a cyclical trade.
bear_case: Memory markets are inherently cyclical; if AI infrastructure spending slows or cloud customers over-order (a recurring pattern in tight supply environments), Micron could face sharp ASP declines and rapid margin compression that unwind the recent price gains.

The stock is at all-time highs with elevated RSI after a massive run; near-term profit-taking risk is significant, and any negative AI demand signal from Big Tech earnings week could trigger a sharp pullback from these stretched valuation levels.
stocks.vs_52wk_high: Above prior high ($506.99); new all-time high set April 27
